displayName: Canada – Student Visa (Study Permit — Outside Canada)
title: Document Checklist
subtitle: Outside Canada · Study Permit Application
policyBanner: SDS & NSE streams officially closed Nov 8, 2024. All applicants now apply through the Regular Study Permit Stream only. Master's / PhD at public DLIs are exempt from the PAL/TAL requirement as of Jan 1, 2026.

items.title: Valid passport — bio data page + any previously issued Canadian visa pages
items.note: Must be valid for at least 6 months beyond intended stay; upload colour scan.

items.title: Digital photo — IRCC specifications (420 × 540 px, white background, neutral expression)
items.note: File must be a recent photo, ≤ 4 MB, JPEG format. Check IRCC photo tool at canada.ca.

items.title: IRCC online profile complete — IMM 5257 / online application signed & reviewed
items.note: All questions answered, no blanks; use "NA" where applicable. Review for consistency with supporting documents.

items.title: Family information form IMM 5707 (or IMM 5645 if applicable)
items.note: Required for all family members. IMM 5645 used for clients with common-law partner or children.

items.title: Original Letter of Acceptance (LOA) — program name, start/end dates, tuition amount
items.note: LOA must be from a Designated Learning Institution (DLI). Conditional acceptance requires conditions met before travel.

items.title: DLI number verified against IRCC's official DLI list at canada.ca
items.note: DLI status can change; confirm the institution remains active at time of submission.

items.title: Program start date allows realistic IRCC processing time
items.note: Current processing times vary by visa office. Plan for 8–16 weeks. Defer intake if needed.

items.title: Provincial / Territorial Attestation Letter (PAL/TAL) — 2026 rules
items.note: Required for undergraduate and PG diploma/certificate students. Obtain from institution. Quebec CAQ attestation from MIFI acts as PAL/TAL.

items.title: PAL/TAL exemption confirmed (Master's or PhD at public DLI, K–12, extension at same DLI)
items.note: As of Jan 1 2026, master's and doctoral students at public DLIs are PAL-exempt. Include note confirming exemption category.

items.title: Proof of living funds: at least CAD $22,895 (Sep 2025 threshold) + first-year tuition + travel
items.note: Updated threshold effective Sept 1 2025. Add ~$4,000/dependent if bringing family.

items.title: Bank statements — minimum 4–6 months showing consistent balance & account holder name
items.note: Officially certified/stamped by bank. Sudden large deposits require explanation letters.

items.title: GIC from participating Canadian financial institution — verify current amount
items.note: GIC still accepted as proof of funds. Confirm amount with institution at purchase.

items.title: Proof of first-year tuition payment (or payment arrangement with institution)
items.note: Receipt from institution or bank transfer record required.

items.title: Sponsor's letter of financial support — signed, notarized
items.note: Include explanation of relationship to applicant.

items.title: Sponsor's supporting documents — ID, ITR (2–3 years), salary slips, employment letter
items.note: Certified English/French translation if in another language.

items.title: Language test result (TRF) — IELTS / CELPIP / PTE / TEF / DALF as required by institution
items.note: Confirm required test & score with institution. IELTS/TEF also required for PGWP at post-graduation stage.

items.title: Academic transcripts — all levels completed, official sealed copies
items.note: Include mark sheets and certificates. Certified translations if not in English or French.

items.title: Degrees / diplomas — originals and copies; certified translations where applicable
items.note: Credential evaluations (e.g. WES) may be required by institution, not IRCC.

items.title: Statement of Purpose (SOP) / Study Plan — genuine student intent & career pathway
items.note: Address why Canada, why this program, ties to home country. Must be original, not a template.

items.title: Confirm program is PGWP-eligible — verify on IRCC list at canada.ca
items.note: Bachelor's, Master's, PhD remain eligible. Private colleges licensing public curriculum are NOT PGWP-eligible.

items.title: Confirm institution type for PGWP — must be public DLI where applicable
items.note: If client intends to work post-graduation, confirm DLI type before enrollment.

items.title: Spouse/partner OWP eligibility noted — limited to spouses of Master's and PhD students
items.note: As of 2024, spouses of undergraduate or diploma students are no longer eligible for OWP.

items.title: Upfront medical examination — IRCC-designated panel physician where required
items.note: Required if studying 6+ months AND from/residing 6+ months in a designated country.

items.title: Biometrics — booked within 30 days of Biometric Instruction Letter (BIL)
items.note: CAD $85 per person, $170 family max. Confirm 10-year validity exemption in IRCC account.

items.title: Police certificate(s) — each country lived 6+ months since age 18
items.note: Requirements vary by visa office. Certificates must be less than 3 months old at submission.

items.title: Study permit application fee paid — CAD $150; receipt saved
items.note: Pay via IRCC secure payment portal. Save transaction ID.

items.title: Biometrics fee paid — CAD $85 / $170 family; receipt saved
items.note: Confirm exemption in IRCC account before paying if biometrics on file.

items.title: Client reviewed, signed, and dated this checklist
Quality review / QA sign-off — documents cross-checked against IRCC requirements
items.note: Verify no expired documents, certified translations, form consistency.

items.title: Application submitted via IRCC online account; confirmation saved
items.note: Save screenshot, UCI, and application number for tracking.
